title: Amazon plans to do an 'impossible' in data centers with its 'Project Houdini'
description: Amazon's Project Houdini initiative aims to significantly reduce data center construction times for AI development. By shifting assembly of server rooms and other key components to factories, the tech giant expects to cut months from build timelines and tens of thousands of labor hours. This modular approach will enable faster deployment of computing capacity to meet surging AI demand.

title: Orbicular’s Ozempic generic gets U.S. FDA tentative nod
description: The product will be marketed and commercialised in the U.S. by Apotex, which is the ANDA applicant, complex peptide-based generics developer Orbicular said in a release.

title: 'Do you regret leaving PSL for IPL?' SL batter put on spot in Pakistan - Watch
description: Mendis, who was signed by Peshawar Zalmi in the PSL player auction in February for PKR 4.2 crore, was speaking after his team’s 76-run win over Lahore Qalandars in Karachi. The 31-year-old scored 74 off 48 balls and was named Player of the Match.

description: The deployment aimed to enhance joint military coordination, improve operational readiness between the armed forces of the two countries, and support security and stability at regional and international levels

description: High-stakes US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded without a ceasefire or broader diplomatic framework after 21 hours. Key differences, particularly Iran's nuclear program, remain unresolved. Despite the setback, both nations signaled a willingness to continue discussions, with Iran urging the US to drop 'excessive demands' and recognize its rights.

description: The collision involved a bus, a truck, and a pickup van; the bus driver may have lost control of the vehicle, leading to the accident, the police say

description: The consignment was taken into custody and handed over to Income Tax officials as the occupants failed to produce valid documents; the seized jewellery had been brought from Mumbai by flight

title: The origin stories of SPAM
description: The months of April and May could well be celebrated as the birth months of spam — those notoriously undesired messages that clog our information highways. This is because the earliest known case of spam took place on May 3, 1978 and the first such case in the Web as we know it now took place on April 12, 1994. A.S.Ganesh turns off his spam filters to give you these two tales…

Haiti stampede: 30 killed at historic Citadelle Laferrière
description: A deadly stampede at Haiti's Citadelle Laferrière on Saturday claimed at least 30 lives as crowds gathered for an annual celebration. Overcrowding and rainfall contributed to the tragedy at the historic fortress, with many young people among the victims. Officials are responding to the scene, and the death toll may increase.

Pro-Palestine rally: Over 500 arrested in London for protesting
description: London police arrested over 500 individuals during a pro-Palestinian demonstration. The arrests were made for showing support for Palestine Action, a group proscribed in the UK. This action follows a High Court ruling that questioned the ban's legality. Organizers criticized the police action, citing the court's decision. The ban on Palestine Action has led to numerous arrests since its inception.

description: The recurring desecration of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar’s statues in the State is being seen as a deliberate attempt to fracture the Dalit-Sikh social fabric. Vikas Vasudeva finds that as Ambedkar Jayanti nears, people from both communities are determined to ensure that divisive forces do not fracture their relationship in a State where Scheduled Castes constitute 32% of the population

'50-second meeting': Samson reveals CSK's turning point after hat-trick of losses
description: Sanju Samson ended his early-season slump with a match-winning unbeaten 115 for Chennai Super Kings against Delhi Capitals. His century, coupled with Ayush Mhatre's 59, powered CSK to 212 and secured their first win of IPL 2026. Samson expressed gratitude for the team's trust and support after the crucial performance.

Himachal has over 300 cases of Section 118 violations pending

Net deletions due to SIR 5.2cr (10%) in 12 states/UTs; 2 crore included in Phase 2
description: Over 2 crore new voters joined electoral rolls across 12 states and UTs during intensive revision, while nearly 5.2 crore names were deleted. This purification process, aimed at ensuring accurate voter lists and preventing bogus voting, resulted in 45.8 crore electors remaining. Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Uttar Pradesh saw the highest purification percentages.

title: U.S., Iran talks extend into second day as strait showdown deepens
description: In a break with precedent, Iranian and U.S. officials were meeting directly, alongside Pakistan, and not speaking through mediators who shuttled between rooms

title: PM Modi writes to parties, seeks support on women's quota bills
description: Prime Minister Modi has urged all party floor leaders to support the unanimous passage of women's reservation bills next week, aiming for implementation by the 2029 elections. The government seeks to enact the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, which will reserve one-third of Lok Sabha and assembly seats for women.

title: Big share of funds for kids' jabs not spent: RTI data
description: Despite significant annual allocations for India's National Immunisation Programme, a substantial portion of funds remains unspent, raising concerns about implementation efficiency. While vaccine coverage has improved, RTI data reveals consistent spending shortfalls, highlighting a need for better fund utilization and accountability in this crucial public health initiative.

title: Assam turnout nears record 86%, highest rise in Muslim-majority districts
description: Assam witnessed a record-breaking voter turnout of nearly 86% in the 2026 assembly polls, the highest in the state's history. The Muslim belt saw the most significant increase in participation. Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma expressed confidence in an NDA victory, citing strong support from the Sanatani community.

title: Maggi to Maruti, war upends production schedules, retail prices
Exclusive | Before Bengal’s SIR row, Maharashtra CEO red-flagged to ECI: Need more time
Avenge Mamata's insult to Murmu, Shah calls upon tribal community
description: Union Minister Amit Shah urged Bengal's tribal communities to vote against TMC, alleging Mamata Banerjee insulted President Droupadi Murmu. Speaking in tribal-dominated districts, Shah promised a "son of soil" CM and inclusion of Kurmali and Rajbanshi in the Eighth Schedule, while also criticizing TMC for corruption and alleged land grabs by infiltrators.

title: London police arrest 523 people at protest over Palestine Action ban
description: The gathering was the first since London’s High Court ruled in February that ​a ban ‌designating the pro-Palestinian group as a terrorist organisation was unlawful

title: What is Anthropic’s Claude Mythos model?
description: Is Mythos really that capable? Why is this LLM not being made available to the public? What is Project Glasswing?

title: 'Going to have big problems': Trump warns China on reports of weapons shipments to Iran
description: Donald Trump asserted US military dominance over Iran, claiming to have dismantled its capabilities and warning China against supplying weapons.  Despite ongoing peace talks mediated by Pakistan, Trump declared victory regardless of a deal, emphasizing America's win and control over key shipping routes.

title: Hyderabad advocate killed as tanker overturns on BMW
description: A senior advocate from Hyderabad, B Srinivasa Rao, tragically died when a fly ash tanker overturned onto his car at the Keesara toll plaza. The tanker reportedly lost control due to brake failure, crushing the BMW. Rescue operations were challenging due to the severe impact.

title: SIR: Dead woman on voters list but husband, son off in Bengal
description: A 77-year-old woman, Mina Jalal, passed away before her name was cleared by the EC for voter registration, an irony as she had voted previously. Her husband and son were excluded from the voter list after adjudication, while other family members qualified. The family, residing in Kolkata for decades, faces harassment and lack of transparency from the EC.

'SIR is a big scam to bring BJP to office'
description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has accused the Election Commission's Special Summary Revision (SIR) of being a "big scam" orchestrated to help the BJP win. She alleged indiscriminate voter deletion, disproportionately affecting Hindus, and claimed over 250 deaths occurred during the exercise.

title: Mamata alleges bid to cancel her nomination in Bhowanipore
description: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee alleged attempts to cancel her Bhowanipore nomination through "false affidavits" filed by "traitors." Two objections, including a name mismatch and allegations of old criminal cases, were rejected. A third issue regarding ballot logistics was also resolved, ensuring her name remained on the first EVM.

title: Manhattan District Attorney investigates sexual assault claims against Swalwell
description: In his denial, ‌U.S. Representative Eric Swalwell noted that the allegations come ahead of the June ‌primary in the governor’s race

title: 'Big khela' coming to Bengal, declares PM Modi
description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi intensified the BJP's Bengal campaign, challenging the TMC's "khela hobe" slogan and framing the polls as a fight for Bengal's identity. He promised to implement the Uniform Civil Code to end appeasement politics and vowed accountability for those who have "played far too long."

title: Vijay's film not leaked from our system: CBFC
description: The Central Board of Film Certification has refuted claims of the Tamil film 'Jana Nayagan' leaking from its system, stating its certification process is secure. The board clarified that films are handed over to producers via a secure KDM mechanism. The alleged leak, which surfaced online, has sparked outrage and calls for stricter anti-piracy measures within the industry.

title: I am enduring pain fighting the party I built brick by bric: PMK founder S. Ramadoss
description: S. Ramadoss says he is not opposing the ‘Mango’ symbol, and contends that it is his symbol and was created by him. Describing the court verdict on the party dispute as ‘different’, he says the  Anbumani faction is making it seem as if the ‘Mango’ symbol and the party did not belong to him

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: Scheduled Tribe communities in delta region hope for redemption from poverty under new government
description: In the absence of accepted identity proof, the Tamil Nadu Adi Dravidar Housing and Development Corporation has been holding back fund allocation to ST families, say activists

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Beyond the spotlight, the voices that power poll campaigns
description: While the candidates command the spotlight on the stage, the voice-over artistes often play a quiet yet significant role in shaping the reach of election campaigns

title: Kalapriya: from a DMK campaigner and fan of MGR to renowned littérateur
description: Kalapriya says that before joining the DMK, MGR was associated with the Congress. Over the time, he surrounded himself with seasoned political minds, including communist leaders K.  Baladhandayutham  and  Kalyanasundaram. He was further supported by experienced figures such as K.A. Krishnaswamy  and Panruti S. Ramachandran. This combination of cinematic influence and political acumen gave him an exceptional grasp of governance as well as mass psychology.

title: Tamil Nadu voter deleted from electoral roll reminds Supreme Court of promise to protect in case of ‘mass exclusion’
description: The petitioner, C. Geetha, said her name had been a mainstay in the State’s electoral roll over the years; she was even intending to contest as an independent candidate in the 51-Uthangarai (SC) Assembly Constituency and 52-Bargur Assembly Constituency in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections in 2026.

Delegates from 22 countries laud India's poll process
description: International delegates from 22 countries lauded India's recent elections in Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry, highlighting impressive voter turnout and meticulous planning. Witnessing firsthand the seamless execution, including EVM usage and webcasting, they described the process as a "true festival of democracy." Delegates also noted the well-organized polling stations with facilities and praised the transparency measures in place.

description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi shared a rare moment of pleasantries and a brief chat at a recent event, sparking interest. Gandhi's presence at the commemorative program for Jyotirao Phule, where he greeted the PM, was noteworthy.

title: Puducherry Assembly election 2026: Polling peaceful in Karaikal amid heavy turnout
Puducherry Assembly election 2026: High enthusiasm, long queues mark polling as the Union Territory shatters record
description: There were no major complaints pertaining to polling process; however, there were allegations of voter inducement and reports of minor scuffles from two constituencies during the early hours of polling.

description: Karur has the highest number of contestants in a single constituency, with 85 candidates in the race; there were 47 candidates in Perambur, including TVK president C. Joseph Vijay

title: Anaicut: Predominantly tribal constituency long plagued by neglect of basic infrastructure
description: Civic amenities such as bitumen roads, water supply, electricity connection and government hospital still remain a distant dream for residents of many tribal hamlets in the hills

title: Allow Suryavanshi time to grow: Kumble
description: Suryavanshi, who turned 15 last month, has carried his stupendous from the ICC u-19 World Cup into IPL 2026, where his audacious strokeplay has captured the imagination of one and all

title: Drinking water scarcity, accidents on highway are the burning problems of Kulithalai constituency
description: Despite being located on the banks of the Cauvery, which is the source of drinking water for several districts, the residents of Kulithalai are stilling fighting for adequate drinking water supply

title: IAF chief meets top US def officials to discuss shared strategic priorities, MQ-9B drones
description: Indian Air Force Chief Air Chief Marshal A P Singh met with US Air Force leadership, including Gen Ken Wilsbach, to bolster defense ties and discuss shared strategic goals. Key topics included the procurement of MQ-9B Sky Guardian drones and enhancing Indo-Pacific cooperation.

title: Environmental stress from quarrying, waste management issues in focus in Kinathukadavu constituency
Starlink satellite disappeared at 560 km above Earth; tech co seeks clarity from Musk's company
description: Starlink satellite 34343 experienced an anomaly, leading to a loss of communication. While Starlink cited an "anomaly," space tech firm LeoLabs reported a "fragment creation event" generating multiple objects. This incident mirrors a similar event with Starlink satellite 35956 in 2025, highlighting ongoing space safety concerns.

title: From the margins to momentum: the BJP’s electoral journey in Tamil Nadu
description: The BJP initially capitalised on Hindutva politics in Kanniyakumari, winning its first Assembly seat in 1996; thereafter it tasted success only in alliance with the Dravidian majors; hopes to convert its rising vote share to more seats in 2026

title: McIlroy sizzles to a record six-shot Masters lead
description: The holder sinks birdies on six of the last seven holes for a epic finish at the midpoint of the year’s first Major

title: Indian LPG tanker crosses Hormuz, first after ceasefire
description: The Indian-flagged LPG tanker Jag Vikram successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first Indian vessel to pass through the conflict zone since a temporary ceasefire. This vessel, carrying 20,400 tonnes of cooking gas, is expected to reach Mumbai on April 15.

title: IPL controversy: RR manager violates protocol by using phone in dugout
description: Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder was seen using a phone in the dugout during an IPL match, violating PMOA rules that ban such use outside the dressing room. The incident, noticed during the chase, has drawn scrutiny, with potential action from officials. IPL authorities stress strict phone-use guidelines to prevent corruption risks, and an investigation may follow.

title: Govt mulls sending surplus rice as aid to conflict-hit Iran
description: India is poised to permit wheat exports to Egypt, Indonesia, and Myanmar due to surplus stocks. Rice shipments, including to war-torn Iran on humanitarian grounds, are also being considered. This move aims to free up storage space for upcoming winter crops and capitalize on increased demand amid global supply chain disruptions.

title: Duty on diesel exports hiked from Rs 21.5/L to Rs 55.5
description: The government has significantly increased export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel to prioritize domestic availability amidst West Asian tensions.  Diesel export duty rose by over 150% to Rs 55.5 per litre, and ATF to Rs 42 per litre, aiming to deter exports and ensure sufficient supply within India.

title: 23-year-old UP woman dies after jilted lover throws acid on her
description: A 23-year-old woman died and her mother is in critical condition after a jilted lover allegedly threw acid on them while they slept. The accused, who had a relationship with the victim, reportedly conspired with an associate to carry out the attack after her marriage was fixed elsewhere. Police have arrested two individuals in connection with the incident.

title: No forum to deal with arbitrators' misdeeds: SC judge
description: Supreme Court Justice B.V. Nagarathna emphasized that arbitration and mediation are vital to a modern justice system, not just alternatives to court. She noted a gap in addressing complaints against arbitrators, often retired judges, whom courts are hesitant to remove.  Maintaining litigant trust is crucial, as bias allegations erode confidence in these dispute resolution methods.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry hit $150K milestone for a cause
description: Stephen Curry, the Golden State Warriors’ star player, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, have always been passionate about helping society and have always been charity driven and trying to help out children from their hometown, Oakland. Despite the wealth and fame that surround the high profile couple, both Stephen Curry and his wife, Ayesha Curry, remain passionate about the causes they believe in.

title: ‘I have failed a lot’: Sanju Samson on his first CSK hundred, the Rajinikanth salute — and why it was all for Fleming

R Vaishali closes in on World Championship berth, Praggnanandhaa knocked out of Candidates title race

Jaishankar discusses West Asia situation with his UAE counterpart
13 dead as speeding bus collides with van in Bihar's Katihar
description: In a devastating accident on National Highway-31 in Katihar district, Bihar, a bus and van collided head-on, resulting in the loss of 13 lives and injuring 22 others. The van was transporting members of the tribal community back from Jharkhand when it was hit by a bus driven by an alleged drunk driver.

title: 6.5 yrs after bifurcation, J&K removed from Aadhaar records of Ladakh residents
description: After a six-year delay, Aadhaar records for Ladakh residents now correctly reflect 'Ladakh' instead of 'Jammu & Kashmir' in the state field. This long-pending correction, facilitated by the UT administration and UIDAI, resolves significant inconveniences faced by residents in accessing services and verifying their identity.

title: J&K: Dogs maul 8-year-old girl to death in Sopore
description: An eight-year-old girl was tragically mauled to death by dogs in Sopore's Seloo village, marking the second such fatality in the area within a year. The child suffered severe injuries in an apple orchard attack. Local residents are shaken, with some alleging dog relocation is exacerbating the problem.

title: IPL: Samson's 115, Overton’s 4-fer help CSK beat DC for season's first win
description: Sanju Samson’s unbeaten 115 powered Chennai Super Kings to their first IPL 2026 win, beating Delhi Capitals by 23 runs. He shared a 113-run stand with Ayush Mhatre (59) to post 212/2. Delhi started strong but lost momentum after early wickets. Jamie Overton’s 4/18 ensured CSK sealed a comfortable victory despite Tristan Stubbs’ fighting 60.

title: What stands out most is Ayush Shetty’s composure… He is handling pressure situations well: Coach Vimal Kumar
Teen sold postcards outside Angkor Wat and spoke 12 languages; Where is he now?
description: A Cambodian teenager, Thuch Salik, gained global fame for his remarkable ability to converse in over a dozen languages while selling postcards at Angkor Wat. This viral talent led to a life-changing scholarship in China, offering him formal education and a brighter future. His story highlights the transformative power of opportunity and resilience.

title: Chennai Super Kings record first win of the season, defeat Delhi Capitals by 23 runs at Chepauk
Hormuz mine clearance, 'some headway' on Lebanon: What we know so far on US-Iran talks
description: The United States and Iran have commenced direct negotiations in Pakistan following a fragile ceasefire, marking a rare face-to-face engagement. Discussions are reportedly making headway, particularly on developments in Lebanon. Key issues on the table include Iran's nuclear program, ballistic missiles, regional influence, and control over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: CUET PG 2026 Answer Keys Out: NTA goes full Dhurandhar with ‘Baccha hai tu mera’ post

What to watch on OTT: Tu Yaa Main, O’ Romeo, The Testaments and more

France to drop Windows: Government to shift to Linux; EU pushes digital sovereignty
description: France is ditching Microsoft Windows for Linux, a significant step in Europe's push for digital sovereignty and reduced reliance on American tech giants. This move, aimed at regaining control over data and digital infrastructure, aligns with broader EU efforts to lessen dependence on foreign technology. The initiative begins with government digital agencies, signaling a strategic shift in national digital policy.

title: US terminates green cards of son, family of Iran hostage crisis figure ‘Screaming Mary’ Ebtekar
Sharad Pawar says regressive elements in Warkari sect, CM says he’s wrong

Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry make noble move, continue giving back
description: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.

title: Dianna Russini’s ‘Miami’ remark sparks outrage; denies Vrabel affair
description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and The New York Times’ popular NFL reporter and insider, Dianna Russini, have sparked outrage among millions after pictures of them sitting together at an upscale hotel were leaked to the media. Both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have strongly denied any rumors of an affair and continue to maintain they were in a professional setting surrounded by other people. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini’s old remarks have raised eyebrows.

title: Who is Jennifer Vrabel? Mike Vrabel’s wife amid affair rumours
description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has sparked outrage as many are convinced that he has allegedly cheated on his wife, Jennifer Vrabel, with The New York Times’ Dianna Russini. While both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have strongly denied affair rumors, the head coach’s wife has stayed out of the spotlight even as they have been married for 27 years.
